ops.body:
Two more instances of the eviction-wedge class observed 2026-07-20/21 during the agent-plane revert work:

1. This laptop: a `0.14.1.upgrading` daemon survived a brew upgrade to a `0.14.2` CLI — every handshake fails with EOF, and the `.upgrading` lifecycle marker never cleared, so the stale daemon is neither evicted nor healthy. Manual kill + marker cleanup required (folded into the v0.16.0 E2E pass as its D0 hygiene step).
2. yasyf-home: two daemons alive after an upgrade race, each bound to a different tailnet leg, serving divergent state (different revisions for the same session id on /api/sessions per leg). Diagnosed live against session 0f6bc33d via both legs.

Both argue the eviction path needs to handle (a) a wedged `.upgrading` incumbent that can't complete its own handshake, and (b) N>1 incumbents, not just one.
